title: alnus acuminata subsp. glabrata
intro: Alnus acuminata subsp. glabrata, also known as the Andean alder or white alder, is a subspecies of tree in the alder family (Betulaceae) native to the Andean region of South America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which help to treat wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Alnus acuminata subsp. glabrata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and provide antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Alnus acuminata subsp. glabrata include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from the bark and leaves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
